event: stayed in the military base camp
never sought medical treatment for his current condition
hypotensive with blood pressure of 81/53 mmHg
tachypnoeic with respiratory rate of 30 breaths per minutes
oxygen saturation of 75-80% on 15L high flow mask
coarse crepitation over both lower zone of his lungs
palpable liver around 5 cm below the costal margin
no cervical, inguinal or axillary lymph nodes palpable
low white blood cell of 0.5 × 106/L with neutrophil predominance (86.1%)
C-reactive protein was markedly elevated at 28.28 mg/dL
Liver function tests were normal except serum albumin of 22 g/dL
Dengue NS-1 Antigen, IgG and IgM antibody were negative
A chest radiograph (Fig. 1) showed consolidation of right upper lobe and left lower lobes
diagnosis of severe community acquired pneumonia (CAP) with acute kidney injury
resuscitated with normal saline at a rate of 20ml/kg bolus
commenced empirically with intravenous ceftriaxone and azithromycin
required intubation and mechanical ventilation in intensive care unit (ICU)
bronchoscopy done on day 2 of admission
copious amount of haemoserous and greenish secretion
right upper bronchus and left main bronchus
repeated chest radiograph showed worsening consolidation in both lung fields
antibiotics were upgraded to intravenous meropenem and cloxacillin
severe metabolic acidosis and oliguric acute kidney injury
deteriorated further with persistent spiking of temperature
refractory hypotension despite maximum dose of multiple inotropic agents
blood cultures, atypical bacterial and Leptospiral serologies were negative
Hepatitis B/C and HIV serologies were undetected
Respiratory viruses screening were also negative
tracheal aspiration and bronchoalveolar lavage found to be positive for MDR Acinetobacter baumannii
susceptible only to polymyxin B with minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 0.5 μg/ml
MDR Acinetobacter baumannii was tested resistance to penicillin group
PCR for carbapenemases genes NDM, OXA-23, OXA 24 or OXA-58 are not routinely performed in our hospital
